description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn attached to a wooden base. The base is rectangular, made of dark-stained wood with visible grain and a slightly weathered finish, and it sits on a flat platform with a protruding crank handle on one side. The turntable platter is circular, dark in color, and occupies the top center of the base, with a metallic tonearm and stylus assembly positioned beside it. The horn is ornate, with a scalloped edge and a golden-bronze patina, curving upward and outward to amplify sound. The tonearm is metallic, dark gray, and rests on the platter, while the crank handle is wooden and attached to the side for manual operation. No modern controls or dust cover are visible, emphasizing its antique design.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and manual crank handle, featuring a dark turntable platter and metallic tonearm.
description_view_0: Side view showing the large brass horn extending upward and outward, the wooden base, and the crank handle on the right.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the back of the horn, the flat wooden base, and the crank handle extending to the left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn extending left, showing the tonearm and platter clearly, and the crank handle on the far side.
description_view_3: Front view directly facing the open mouth of the horn, with the wooden base and turntable platter centered below.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and dark in color, likely covered with felt or a similar material. It occupies the central top area of the wooden base and is proportionally smaller than the base but large enough to hold a standard record. The surface appears smooth with no visible grooves or markings, and it has a matte finish.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and dark gray, with a curved shape. It is of moderate length, positioned to the side of the platter, and appears to be resting on or near the platter. The tonearm has a visible joint where it connects to the base, allowing for movement.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present, attached to the end of the tonearm. It is small, metallic, and appears as a distinct component at the tip of the tonearm, designed to contact the record surface.
